reviews.review_description: One the jankiest game i ever played on this, i haven't Even played for two hours and i can't refund

I downloaded this game when it first dropped and was still in beta. It has done nothing but get better ever since. I'm not going to lie when it first dropped even in beta, was one of my top favorite games to play on here. Easily spent the most time in VR on this game right here. Mad respect to the developer and designer of this game.....

                    .You killed it with this.

I never write reviews but this deserves it. First of all, there’s like no types of zombies, gore is terrible, I try to get the map up it disappears after like 3 milliseconds and lastly everything feels janky and climbing feels terrible.

The things are extractions are nice it’s good that you have to stab a sword in and then further, gun reloads are quite nice the recoil is good hands look cool.

I love how ot feel to get that satisfaction of a clean head cut off but i was honestly disappointed that ot single player do get me wrong it's and amazing game and it's better then the frist but it really needs multiplayer

Every run feels a bit different, which makes the game easy to replay. The act combat stays engaging and the progression gives you a reason to jump back in. A really nice pick if you enjoy roguelike shooters.

reviews.review_title: Want to love this game.want a refund
reviews.review_description: I even played it past the refund stage because i really want it to work. The instances of it working i feel like real cyberpunk. Also see a lot of potential for a remake or part two that’s multiplayer with explorable areas and mini games like a functional dart board even. I so badly want to be a cyberpunk in vr this game would be scratch that itch if it worked and if it was expanded on. I

reviews.review_title: Bro, this game is honestly kinda fire
reviews.review_description: The whole cyberpunk look in VR just hits different and smashing zombies feels super fun. The weapons feel great, the visuals pop and it great you hyped real quick. If you’re into futuristic stuff this one’s definitely worth a shot.

Really want to like this and it does have interesting moments but...
It doesn't feel finished. Enemies - even just visually - lack variety. Sound lacks variety. Maps and rooms lack cyberpunk pop and flash. Reloading is very clunky. Where's the reload options? Why the overly small map doesn't work with a weapon in hand? The initial onboarding misses the absolute essentials: where's the extraction point? (It's actually the START POINT!!?!) Roguelite but does the level unlocking really have to reset? Sword kills in a few swipes so why does it take so much shooting to kill anything? The spiders feel overly fiddly to kill.
But much is there. Weapons and upgrades. Procedural levels. Potential. But I think it's been prematurely abandoned before the core game loop got to be fun and the levels moreish.
Judt started but look good
This game feels really fun in VR. The guns react nicely,  the pacing keeps you active, and the whole experience is just exciting to play. As someone who loves FPS games I enjoyed it a lot.

This VR game instantly pulls you into a dark cyberpunk universe where every corridor feels alive with neon lights and danger around each corner. The randomly generated levels keep each run fresh, so you never know what’s waiting ahead.

The visuals look great in VR, and the combat feels intense — especially when you’re surrounded by zombies in those tight, glowing hallways. Weapon upgrades and smooth controls make the experience even more engaging.

If you enjoy cyberpunk vibes and action-packed VR shooters, this one’s absolutely worth trying.

reviews.review_description:
So I know I'm probably beating a dead horse here, but I seem to remember very strong hints towards multiplayer being in this game.. and I'm looking through the reviews in the developers response is, pretty much, that there isn't going to be a multiplayer? now, I can't remember exactly what it was because it was a while ago and I don't I didn't take screenshots of whatever was telling me that you guys were working on multiplayer..besides, you know, the Social Hub in the game. but besides that I'm fairly certain you guys hinted towards multiplayer in another way whether it was in the description or whatever, but now you're telling people that you guys never promised it and that you're sorry if people were expecting something that "wasn't part of the game". Well either way the Social Hub that was said to be under construction in the lobby of this game very much told us that it was going to be a co-op game. Just own up to your faults and admit that you just don't want to do it or you can't do it. don't blame us for you guys not developing multiplayer for this game that is probably abandoned at this point tbh




EDIT: literally took two seconds of investigation to find your roadmap that says "multiplayer" on it. Pathetic. DONT SUPPORT THIS GAME, NOR THESE DEVELOPERS. LYING DEVS ARE BAD DEVS

developer_response.body:
Hi FetusSupreme,

Thank you for sharing your thoughts.

We understand your frustration and want to clarify this situation openly. Early in development, the roadmap did mention a multiplayer concept — it was something we explored during prototyping. However, as the project evolved, we decided to focus on delivering a full single-player story experience first, as the scope and technical requirements for co-op turned out to be much larger than expected for a small VR team.

From the very first release, the game unfortunately didn’t have a large enough player base to justify focusing on multiplayer development. That’s why we decided to put our efforts into improving and expanding the single-player experience instead. Everything that was officially announced in the roadmap with confirmed dates was completed — and even earlier than planned. The idea of adding multiplayer or game mods only made sense with a large active community, which, sadly, wasn’t the case.

The Social Hub in the lobby was originally designed as a shared space concept, but it never reached the multiplayer stage. We didn’t remove it from the environment because it still represents an in-game lore element tied to future story content, not online play.

We’re not hiding anything — and we definitely don’t want to “blame players.” We truly appreciate everyone who supported the game and gave feedback through these years. 

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio VR

reviews.review_description: Plays alright, but hallways, computer terminals, character portraits are blatantly bad looking generative AI. Even low effort art would be less distracting.
Can we please have Co op for this game? My son and I loved playing Deaath horizon in co op. Would be great to see this in co op
It feels like Cyberpunk 2077 but in vr, really fun :)

I did get stuck in a door. The very first one that goes into reactor/generator room. Other than that a good game. Not one to kill as many zombies as you can, but more of a survival get in and get out. 
The retrieval was cool. Could have used a warning while I was getting dragged backwards and not know why, which ensued a barrage of bad words. Thought it was glitching out.
This one surprise me - great vibes, smooth gameplay and that dark futuristic energy that just pulls you in. Totally worth it.

reviews.review_title: Brutal Addictive with Tons of potential
reviews.review_description:
Honestly I dont usually write reviews but Death Horizon Cyberfusion really deserves one I went all in unlocked every weapon got all the achievements finished every run and even after that I still wanted to keep playing Its one of those games that just feels right in VR

From the start I was hooked The atmosphere the physical combat the cyber upgrades its a solid mix of fast action and sci fi vibes and its clear the devs put a lot of love into it

A few things I think could improve

Weapon slot system the current setup for storing your pistol and katana feels a bit rigid It would be great if we could swap items between slots during a run or just have more flexible inventory options

Sometimes I would have 100 bullets use a 60 round mag and be left with 40 rounds but the game wouldnt let me reload with the leftover bullets That breaks the flow a bit and doesnt make much sense

The final rooms after a full run are confusing big empty spaces where nothing happens Not sure if Im missing something but sometimes you drop down and cant even get back out

Theres a room next to the scoreboard in the main lobby that looks like it should have a purpose but it doesnt Nothing to interact with Makes me wonder if its for future content

I would love to see more map variety and enemy types The layout changes each time but visually it still feels like the same area And while the enemies are solid after a while you know exactly how to deal with them


I noticed the ingame roadmap only goes up to this year so Im not sure if more updates are coming but I really hope they are Because this game has so much potential and with a few tweaks and fresh content it could easily become one of the top VR action titles out there


Final thoughts

I had a blast with this game I genuinely enjoyed every run and even after completing everything I didnt feel done Its got heart great ideas and its already super fun just needs a little polish in a few areas

I truly hope the devs keep going with this project It has everything it needs to keep growing into something even bigger

reviews.review_description:
.this game is fun in many ways, and can obviously be improved. for a game in early access it’s a very solid zombie shooter with an objective and an extraction. the guns shoot well and the reloading is a little finicky (controllers usually always slam against each other when reloading, otherwise the clip just won’t go in or i do it slowly) and the progression system is very smooth in terms of having to grind for something.

the games most troublesome mechanics that i had trouble with was the melee system, the holstering system, and two handing weapons.
when you use your sword on an enemy, most of the time it will just phase through them into their body rather than a slice, even when you mean to decapitate and swing hard. two handing the sword makes it worse, as for any weapon that doesn’t require two hands. it’s harder to control, despite the fact it’s supposed to be easier and it just doesn’t swing properly regardless of your hand positioning.

the holsters are very customizable which is very good, however the holsters are not very good when in use. you will find yourself constantly double backing for your weapons because you MUST look down for your weapon to actually go into your hand. same thing with putting it in the holster, when you drop it in your holster you expect it to stay, instead the weapon is just gone and you must either search on the floor for it or wait for it to teleport back to your holster. while this doesn’t ruin immersion in any way, it ruins the core gameplay where you can use different weapons and combine it for a certain playstyle, as it’s difficult to switch between weapons.

an additional thing i forgot to mention is the map generation or “generation”. while the concept is cool, it doesn’t have any place in a game like this where you have to extract. it makes very long hallways to extend the mission time when you could implement a missions mode, and a set roguelike mode. this makes it work because the missions wouldn’t be extra long, have a chance to be designed and complex, rather than missions being randomized and only a couple of them available. i understand that this isnt the most ideal way, but personally i would love to play a roguelike mode in this game specifically where it’s endless and permadeath.

on a final note, this game reminds me heavily of half life alyx with the grabbing, enemy drones, and weapon customizations. the first death horizon game had an intriguing story with good gameplay which makes me excited for when this game gets one.
 thanks devs!

developer_response.body:
Hi novaked, 

Thank you for such a detailed comment.

We really appreciate your thorough feedback — it helps us a lot. 

We’ll review your notes on melee combat, holstering, two-handing, and mission structure when planning future updates. 

If you have any more ideas or questions, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com

reviews.review_description: I really want to request a refund. However, I genuinely want to support this Dev. But what happened is sooooo frustrating I almost through my oculus out the window. Granted the levels are super redundant. It feels like the very same level over and over and over. Just new objectives in the same are. But ok ill give it a go.  I played like a God!!! Level 1... level 2... Level 3. Played all the way to Level 5 and died on  the 2nd boss. Ok no problem. Ill run it back and get busy..... NOPE! THE GAME RESTARTED MY PROGRESS ALLLLL OVER.  I have to start all the way back to Level 1 and grind my way back to where I died at. Come on man. Why would you guys set the game up like this. Freaky HORRIBLE.  Ive never in my entire life played a game that does this. All of my guns and coins are there. Everything i purchase is available. My Level is still at 10. But i have to START THE MISSION ALL OVER??? No way!!! Wtf is up with this?

developer_response.body:
Hi ZeusDues,

Thank you so much for the detailed feedback — and for wanting to support us even after such a frustrating experience. We’re genuinely sorry it made you feel this way.

The issue you're describing sounds like a progression system misunderstanding or a possible bug — your character level and items are saved, but mission progress resets. That’s not how it’s supposed to feel, and we completely get how frustrating that can be, especially after grinding through multiple levels.

Your feedback really helps us prioritize what needs to be fixed first.

If you have any more thoughts or ideas, feel free to email us at info@dream-dev.com — we’re always listening.

Thanks again for giving the game a shot — we’ll do our best to make sure it's worth your support.

reviews.review_title: Amazing game, just needs a little polish
reviews.review_description:
Cyber fusion is an amazing game, fantastic zombies and tech enemies. The game escalated at a great pace. The weapons are good and fun to use. 

The game itself needs a bit more work, some minor glitches with environmental structures with floors and ceilings. 

For all that keeps you sane once you pick up the briefcase never drop it. I've had it fall into the floor where you can't pick it up or put it down to kill enemies and have it fall into a basement level where your not getting this briefcase back up due to needing to climb ladders and unable to climb with one arm... Maybe being able to store the object in inventory would fix this minor but irritating issue. 

Also would love an icon on the wrist to show you've a medic pack stored and where you stored it.. would have kept me alive a bit longer.

Otherwise a fun game. I love the graphics and overall vibe. Please keep up the great work Devs.

developer_response.body:
Hi Atzroth,

Thank you for the kind words and thoughtful feedback!

We’re happy you enjoyed the game, and we’ve noted your points about the briefcase, climbing, and medic pack indicator. These are valuable suggestions, and we’ll definitely take them into account for future updates.

If you have more ideas, feel free to write us at info@dream-dev.com — and thanks again for playing Death Horizon: Cyberfusion!

reviews.review_description:
The game itself is fun and has lots of potential but room for improvement.  The game has a tendency to get stuck especially post completion of a level.  

I have experienced issues reloading my weapons even though ammo has been collected.  

I definitely enjoy the exploration aspect and the different zombies/ drones. 

A good start but definitely needs some tweeks and improvements.

developer_response.body:
Hi Darkness70,

Thanks a lot for your thoughtful feedback!

We’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the exploration and enemy variety — that’s a big part of the experience we’re aiming for. As for the issues you mentioned — like getting stuck after completing a level or reloading problems — we’re actively working on fixes and improvements to make things smoother and more stable.

We truly appreciate you sticking with us through the early builds. If you have more suggestions or run into other bugs, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com — your feedback helps us shape the future of the game.

Thanks again for playing Death Horizon: Cyberfusion!

reviews.review_description:
Loved the previous Death Horizon game on the Quest 2, and this one looked off to a great start. Ten minutes in, it is FRUSTRATING.

- Placing your sidearms requires you to stop, and look down. It’s very particular, and your body trails behind you when you walk, making it very difficult to put your weapon downs on the go without looking down. 

- Pistol aiming with two hands reduces your ability to move your hands. I’ve never seen a shooter do this before, very unintuitive and frustrating.

- Punching enemies requires you to be point blank and it’s very literal with where your hands are. I feel like other games also provide some grace room with where you can make contact. You’ll often punch your arm forward and come short from hitting 

Generally, there are lots of instances where simple design choices will actively work against you trying to immerse yourself into the game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful feedback, Gecko-VR — and for giving Cyberfusion a try after enjoying the original! 🙏

We hear you. Holstering, pistol handling, and melee accuracy are all super important for immersion, and we agree they need more polish. Your points are clear and helpful, and we’re actively working to improve the overall feel and flow of gameplay.

Thanks again for sharing — and if you’ve got more ideas or frustrations to pass along, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com. 

We're on it!

It would also be cool if they added more cyberware like a cyberware that only slows down time but not for you so you can move the same speed you normally would but the zombies and bosses would be slowed down.

Thanks again for the inspiration — and if more ideas come to mind, hit us up at info@dream-dev.com!

reviews.review_description:
Let's start by saying that this game is fun and has huge potential but as always in life let's focus on negative first. There are many bugs in the game to name a few :
- aiming with two handed weapon is horrible 
- Sword sometime stay stuck or is just thrown away
- Hand stay in the terminal and is as from rubber
- weapon holstering is clunky
- enemy movement should be improved - environment looks just horrible we are definitely missing quest 3 update. 

That being said I will not refund because this is the only cyberpunk game in quest with great music and good feel. please improve the game and it can be a real gem. 

Once fixed 5 stars no question.

Update
Some fixes are there. Environment improvement there. We are still missing spawns with animations and more things to do after level 15. I have encountered some bugs with artefact collections... 4 stars for now

6th march 2025 update
Game is too easy. Terminus did not solve that. Nothing to do after level 15.
Arms still get stuck in terminals.
Two handed weapons improved/solved.
Zombie spawning without animations
Biggest issue. From beginning there is this stupid texture redrawing that is really getting on nerves. Dungeons of eternity that uses much bigger rooms does not do that. This should be removed for quest 3 at least.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your thoughtful and detailed feedback, RadkoVan!

We really appreciate your support and the fact that you’re sticking with the game — even through the rough edges.

Your feedback helps us push in the right direction, and we truly hope the next updates bring the game closer to that 5-star experience.

If you ever want to share more, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com! 💪

reviews.review_title: So close to being a favorite
reviews.review_description: The game feels like a mixed bag. The graphics are amazing even on quest 2, but the environments feel empty. The guns generally feel alright, but the reloads feel sluggish and uncomfortable. Not to mention trying to use 2 hands on any gun and how unresponsive it feels compared to Reloaded. The Gorrila power feels great, the others not so much. The missions encourage running from point A to B instead of exploring and fighting. The only really fun I had was sector 5 and the bosses, but even then the fact you have to leave and come back to fully clear the zone is less than ideal. The upgrades give a good sense of progression, but it takes so long to get them that I completed the game twice before hitting level 9.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback, Cerealman125! 🙏

We’re glad you enjoyed the graphics and some of the missions — and it’s great to hear that Sector 5 and the bosses stood out!

That said, we hear you on the issues with reload mechanics, two-handed weapons, and mission structure. We're already working on improving responsiveness and making combat and progression more engaging across the board.

Your feedback is incredibly valuable, and we hope future updates will bring the experience closer to your expectations.

Feel free to share more thoughts anytime at info@dream-dev.com!

Your input is valuable—if you have any other ideas, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com.

reviews.review_description:
Here’s a short list of improvements that I think would make the game even better:

1. Auto Height Setting: The auto height feature needs improvement. It should save the setting so I don’t have to adjust it every time I start a new round.


2. Melee Weapons: Consider replacing the standard straight blade with something more varied, like a broadsword or an axe, to add more excitement to melee combat.


3. Dual Wielding Pistols: Reintroducing the ability to dual wield pistols from the first game would be a significant improvement. Adding a quick tap-to-chest reload mechanic would further enhance the gameplay experience.


4. Item Placement: Currently, item placement is inconsistent—items either phase into or are pushed out of the body depending on the player’s movement direction. A fixed, stationary item placement system would make it easier to grab items reliably.


5. Weapon Reload Mechanics: I’ve noticed some issues with racking weapons. Extending the weapon rack aperture or expanding the field of grab would help avoid awkward hand positioning and make reloading more fluid.


6. Climbing Mechanics: The climbing mechanics need to be improved. While being attacked by a horde of zombies, I struggled to climb out of a room due to how sluggish the mechanics feel compared to the first game. Faster, more responsive climbing would greatly enhance the overall experience.



Overall, it’s a fantastic game, and I had a lot of fun playing it!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi Ogoferael,

Thank you for your detailed feedback! We’ll discuss it with the team and do our best to include it in upcoming updates.

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_title: This game NEEDS A LOT of work
reviews.review_description:
First, let me say that the developers are great! I love what they do, and I enjoy supporting their work.
Value for price here is good, but! In 2025, after playing countless games with more polished mechanics, I just can't give more than 2/5 for this game, and EVEN that is generous.

1. Graphics, the devs done great job with the environments, but boy! those limbs (hand & body), zombie assets and the blood could definitely be more polished. they are so clunky that they completely ruin the immersion in game.

2. Gameplay, probably the biggest downfall and what made me stop playing and wait for the devs to push out more update before i resume. it is just NOT fun to play. it feels more like a rouge like game rather than a game where you go from point A to point B. im just going around asking myself, whats the point apart of collecting stuff to unlock more stuff to collect later on?

3. Mechanics, oh dear lord.....what happened here exactly? i know you can do better than that, you already done better than that. the melee in Death Horizon is so much better in comparison that i had hard time believing that the same team worked on both games.
the fists when you punch, there is no "umph" i cant even seem to connect with other characters before they blow away.
the sword is ridiculously bad, only one way to grab it and it has no weight. not only that, a lot of times when you hit a zombie it gets stuck and you lose the grip. if this is intended then at least let me grab it back, instead its just stuck there. the angle on which it sits on the body is also awkward, i.e its just hangs vertically instead of in a diagonal position like a sword should be carried.
the blades in hands are similar to the fists, i.e you cant feel real connection when hitting characters with them.

i could go on, but unfortunately thats the limit of reviews Meta allows. Devs, i love you guys, please dont be offended. i know you can do much much better that this and i am sure you will in future updates.

developer_response.body:
Hi pikpukpak,

Thank you for your detailed feedback and for supporting our work. We appreciate your input and are committed to improving the overall game experience. Your suggestions are valuable, and our team is actively working on enhancements that we hope will address many of your concerns.

If you have any additional ideas, please feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com.

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_description: Great idea but the game itself is absolute garbage I couldn't play more than 10 minutes

If you’re open to sharing what specifically didn’t work for you, we’d love to hear it — just drop us a line at info@dream-dev.com. 

Your feedback can help us make the game better.

reviews.review_description:

So far so good gunplay very responsive, grabbing objects like sword once dropped and throwing it in the air to catch is soooo 😎 

Just overly satisfying chopping zombies heads off 👍 

All I can say is don't let the cheap price put you off its a wee hidden ✨️ 

Well it's certainly opened my 👀 to try others I thought I'd never give a chance

💯% worth a go I just hope the team is still working on this one 🙏

reviews.review_title: Cool game with some annoying flaws
reviews.review_description:
The sword constantly gets stuck in the zombies no matter how hard you swing, can’t just chop their heads off/chop them in half, then you have to role play king arthur pulling the sword from the stone to get it out as you get mogged by all the other zombies. Very frustrating, just look silly swinging the sword around with a dead zombie on the end of it.

then there’s the insanely long and equally empty hallways, for absolutely no reason. I have never in my life experienced motion sickness until I played this game. I quite literally used to live on a sailing ship, it was my summer job in high school where I was a deckhand. I also grew up on boats as my father is a lobster fisherman. Playing this game for 20 minutes made me feel queasy.

If the sword could chop though zombies, and the hallways were shortened like 80%, or even taken out just make it room to room, this game would easily be 4.5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Hi Rogue_Cheeks,

Thank you for sharing your feedback with us.

Regarding the sword:

If you strike a zombie in the head with at least medium force, it will be decapitated with one blow. If you strike the body or deliver a weaker hit, the sword can get stuck, requiring extra effort to cut all the way through. This is intentional gameplay, not a bug. If every swing passed through the enemy regardless of force, there’d be no reason to aim for powerful strikes, and it would feel unrealistic overall.

If you have more suggestions or questions, feel free to email us at info@dream-dev.com.

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_description:
Although the graphics are okay and the cyberpunk aspect is pretty cool. 
The combat is pretty bad and the normal melee weapons don't really do much. I like how the enemies are always coming but at the same time, it's a double sided sword because you don't get enough time to prep. Another thing I find kinda annoying about this game is that there's honestly no real blocking mechanic and your hotbox is pretty big for what it should be, so dodging is almost impossible. 
The one thing that really annoys me is that my height is always resetting to default when I load into an area and I have to fix it every time.

developer_response.body:
Hi DROID2X,

Thank you for your feedback. We appreciate your points about melee combat, constant enemy spawns, dodging/blocking, and the height reset issue. We’re currently working on improvements to address these concerns, so stay tuned for future updates.

If you have any more suggestions, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com.

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_description: Randomly generated facility, extraction based gameplay, and nice gun mechanics as well as fantastic melee combat. Tons of replayability with the randomized maps and missions within the maps. The road map ahead looks very promising for making the absolute best Zombie game on quest.

We’re thrilled to hear that Cyberfusion aligns with your dream zombie game experience!  We’re excited to keep working toward our roadmap and making this the best zombie game on Quest.

If you have any suggestions or ideas, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com. Thanks again for your support!

Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_description:
Even though I don't like roguelikes at all, this game completely gets me. The weapon selection and modification options are great and the game stages are exciting, stressful and action-packed.
Only the strength of the weapons leaves something to be desired and my katana often gets stuck somewhere and I lose it, which can be really tragic in the heat of battle. It takes a lot of hits to break a zombie, but a zombie bursts like an overripe tomato - which makes me wonder about the quality of my sword.
Unfortunately, the map is not always displayed correctly on the wrist and the section is extremely small.
But what bothers me most is that I never know exactly what I actually have to do. Rather, I wander through the corridors and deal with everything that comes my way, only to then rush back to the elevator after a while. Some places on the map are marked with ? or ! provided, but I rarely find anything there.
The great action and oppressive atmosphere with great soundscape are so much fun for me that I can only enjoy the game.
A multiplayer would be the necessary icing on the cake!

developer_response.body:
Hi LordValFromHell,

Thank you very much for the detailed feedback. We’re already discussing it with the team. 

If you have additional suggestions or ideas, feel free to email us at info@dream-dev.com.

Regards,
Dream Dev Studio.

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed the first game "death horizon reloaded" so when I saw this game by the same people for sale, I just had to buy it. I'm glad that I did and for an early access title it is very promising. You can see in the main hub the updates that are planned for this game and just waiting for the updates is enough excitement in itself. Now on to the game: the developers have managed to do it again and make a game that once you start playing find hard to put down. There are certain areas of the game where one wishes it was co-op (a definite future update I'm sure) but despite that it still is a good game solo. As with all new games, there are some glitches and bugs but nothing game breaking. If you played the first game as much as I have then this game is a no-brainer and you should purchase as soon as possible. And hey... it's zombie killing galore; never gets old.

developer_response.body:
Hi Mobey561,

Thank you so much for your awesome review and for being a fan of Death Horizon Reloaded!

We’re thrilled to hear that you’re enjoying Cyberfusion and excited about what’s to come. We're actively working on the planned updates you mentioned. While we’re aware of some minor glitches, we’re working hard to smooth those out in upcoming patches.

If you have any ideas or feedback, feel free to reach out at info@dream-dev.com. Thanks for your support, and happy zombie hunting!

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

Have to drop by and give a review for this awesome game. it sucks that I have had to refund a lot of games lately because they feel unfinished or simply like they released five years ago. That’s not the case with this game. It has that polished feel that is lacking in some recent releases. I love the cyberpunk twist too. 

I hope other developers take note and make games that feel more like this and less like the first five years that VR launched. ￼￼

reviews.review_description:
The physics are really nice and once you are surrounded by the undead while your Katana ist still stuck in, let's say, a melon, this will get your blood pumping. That said, Cyberfusion doesn't treat you to breaks. Even activating a terminal can mean sudden death, if you don't have 360° hawk's eyes.

The weapon upgrades are interesting and creative and the melees are a joy to perform. The music is very fitting and carries that special Cyberpunk feel of aggressive hopelessness and the loss of the human soul through technological advancements.

Who's a good Cyberpsycho? You are!

reviews.review_description: The game is a fun roguelike but has a lot of bugs. I’m surprised it was released in its current state. From getting thrown across the room when hitting a “zombie” with your sword, to lack of working height adjustments that reset once you enter a level so that you are always crouching. You cannot even adjust the height from your menus but only in the lobby. But the biggest complaint is locking the assault rifles when using both hands. The gun is impossible to aim unless you aim first and then steady the weapon with your non-dominant hand. This locks the weapon and you cannot “tilt” the barrel, so that if you grab it prior to aiming down the sights you cannot shoulder the weapon to aim down the sights. Very poor decision to release like this. But the price is cheap enough and the developers are known for patching their games so hopefully this gets more polish soon.

developer_response.body:
Hi ShadowBane79,

Thank you for your detailed feedback!

We appreciate your honesty and apologize for the bugs you've encountered. Rest assured, we’re actively working on new updates to improve the game.

Your feedback is invaluable as we continue refining the game, and we aim to resolve these issues in future updates.

Regarding "locking the assault rifles when using both hands," could you please provide us with more details by emailing us at info@dream-dev.com? We want to make sure we fully understand your idea.

Thank you again!

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_description: This game has a solid set of Bones under it. I'm giving it a three-star rating right now understanding it's an early access game and is going to have a lot of development in the future. All the basic bones are there though for a great game. My major issue is with the katana right now which I know is being addressed so I'm not going to belabor the point. I would like to see some verticality in the game later on where you can climb. one of the best things about the original death Horizon was the climbing mechanic was so solid. I've noticed that weapon attachments can be pulled off very easily during Gunplay maybe implement a function where you can't pull them off without pressing buttons or turning the weapon in a certain direction would be a good idea.

developer_response.body:
Hi mekillsondeck,

Thank you for your comment.

I'd like to inform you that we have uploaded the first game update.

If this update has resolved some of your issues, we would be grateful if you could take a minute to update your review.

Additionally, if you encounter any problems with the game or have any ideas on how we can improve it, please don't hesitate to contact us at info@dream-dev.com.

Thank you!

Update #1, version 0.1.7:

- Changed the angle for the Energy Blade (Katana) with the possibility to set up the angle manually in the game options.
- Changed the angle for firearms.
- Improved the grab system, which should resolve the issue where it was hard to pick up objects in some situations.
- Added game options to the Pause menu.
- Added additional physical objects to the game levels with which you can interact and use to defeat enemies.
- Added the option to change the volume of the player's voiceover in the game menu.
- Music battle state is now stable and no longer interrupted randomly.
- Music mix has been enhanced.

In upcoming updates:

- We will add a new weapon: the shotgun.
- We will improve the inventory system with the ability to place any object in any slot.
- We are working on improving game graphics.
- We will add additional tasks to the game missions.
- We will add more variety to the level design.
- We are working on improving game stability.

----

reviews.review_title: Promising Premise, but I'm Not Sure..
reviews.review_description:
I enjoy Death Horizon: Reloaded (what I've played of it so far at least), but this one seems to be shallower. There seems to be nothing outside of jumping into a map and completing the same simple tasks, so you can get credits, level up, and get upgrades to get better at completing the tasks so you can get credits, level up more, and get upgrades to get better at repeatedly doing the same things with no other reward or incentives.
I'm no fan of Rogue-likes for this reason (despise the lazy genre even), but this doesn't seem the worst in that regard either.
One thing that does get to me though is the difficulty of it (as with all Rogue-likes, being overly đ unevenly difficult if not unfair to KEEP your chances of dying much higher so you HAVE to lose everything/ all your hard earned progress to pad the game's runtime rather than actual new content with actual substance...).  The zombies & annoying bug creatures never stop flooding in and they all are complete bullet sponges, and the sword only does so much if there are more than 1 or 2 (and about useless against the kamakazi bugs eating away at your limited health unfairly). So I've yet to be able to get past a single map or complete a single task. Or even find out HOW to complete/ find the given tasks due to always being swarmed. I'd otherwise actually have fun with it, but as is it's just impossible/ too frustrating for me to enjoy.
So the combat just doesn't feel the absolute greatest to me, and that's basically all there is to the game...
The premise being so promising but being wasted on an arcadey rogue-like is disappointing (as is normally the case..), but it seems great for those who are into that.
So I definitely recommend it for anyone that just want to kill hoards of zombies with a huge Sci-gi twist to everything.

developer_response.body:
Hi Teele891,

Thank you very much for your detailed review!

Yes, you're right—Death Horizon: Reloaded and Death Horizon: Cyberfusion are different games. Cyberfusion is not a continuation of Reloaded or anything like that.

The main focus of Cyberfusion is action. It's not about the story or progressing from beginning to end. Instead, it's more about the challenge: defeating as many enemies as you can, earning credits, retreating from the level if things get too tough, upgrading your weapons in the game HUB, returning to a new level, defeating more enemies, buying new weapons, and repeating the process.

If you're finding the game difficult, here are some tips:

1. Move quickly. The longer you stay in the level, the stronger the enemies become.
2. It's better to retreat when your health is low than to die and lose everything.
3. Look for coins in the levels by accessing terminals.
4. Upgrade your weapons in the HUB. Start by purchasing the rifle—it's much more powerful than the pistol.
5. Use your hands to beat enemies—it's easy and fun with Gorilla implants!
6. Remember, you can interact with enemies—push them, or throw objects from the level at them.
7. Aim for enemies' weak points to defeat them faster.
8. Train in the HUB with different types of enemies before starting a mission.

And always remember, you can reach out to us anytime at info@dream-dev.com if you have any questions, get stuck, or need help with anything.

We hope that with these tips, you'll give the game another shot and enjoy it even more!

Thanks again for your review.

Best regards,
Dream Dev Studio

reviews.review_description: Not playable. Sword gets stuck in zombies, frustrating game. Needs a bunch of fixes I’m returning the game played for one hour. Can’t do it any longer.￼

developer_response.body:
Hi Dec_DentalDave,

Thank you for your comment.

You might not believe it, but in real life, swords can also get stuck in the body. To fully cut through, you need to keep pushing the sword in the same direction as the strike. This way, you can slice through completely.

Also, we’re excited to share that a new update with additional features and improved mechanics will be available soon.

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact us at info@dream-dev.com.

Thank you.

reviews.review_description:
Great job so far. There’s still some room for improvement, but the game is great straight out of the gate. The gameplay is fun, the graphics are smooth, the audio is on point, the controls are intuitive, and I didn’t experience any bugs with the exception of 1 mag falling through the floor. Plenty of upgradable weapons to play with that do all sorts of different things. And plenty of enemies to battle. Congrats to the devs for creating this great game. I’m excited to see what’s in store for the future.
I received this game for free via the Facebook group, Meta Quest Promotions, Giveaways and Referrals. Thanks to Andy, Paul and Mark, the administrators of that Facebook group, for all they do to spread the word about Quest games/apps.
